output.answer: Wikidata does not contain the latest version of Chrome for Linux in its current data. No results were found using standard version properties (P119, P2415) in the Chrome (Q508825) entity.
output.formatted:
Wikidata does not contain the latest version of Chrome for Linux in its current data. No results were found using standard version properties (P119, P2415) in the Chrome (Q508825) entity.

SPARQL query over wikidata:
```sparql
PREFIX wdt: <http://www.wikidata.org/prop/direct/>
PREFIX wd: <http://www.wikidata.org/entity/>
SELECT ?version WHERE {
  wd:Q508825 wdt:P119 ?version
}
```

Execution result:
Got no rows and 1 columns

content.message:
The search for the latest version of Chrome for Linux in Wikidata has been attempted using multiple properties (P119, P2415, and statement-based queries), but all returned no results. This indicates that Wikidata does not currently store the latest version number for Google Chrome on Linux as part of its structured data.

The most direct approach using the standard version property (`wdt:P119`) failed to return any data, and no alternative property mappings for the latest version were identified. Since Wikidata typically contains static, community-edited information and may not reflect the most recent software releases in real time, it is unlikely to have this specific detail.

Thus, the answer is that the latest version of Chrome for Linux is not available in Wikidata based on the current data.
